title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a teacher giving information about a school theatre trip.
audio_script: Speaker: Listen carefully, everyone, because I need to give you the final details for Friday's theatre trip. We are going to see a comedy at the Royal Theatre, and the performance is suitable for all students in Year Ten. Your group leader for the trip will be Miss [14]Foster[/14], so please go to her if you have any problems during the evening. We will leave school at [15]five twenty[/15], not five thirty as it says on the old letter, because the traffic can be busy near the theatre. You should bring your school ID card and a small [16]snack[/16] for the coach journey. Please do not bring hot food, as it is not allowed on the coach. The play is called The [17]Lost Ticket[/17], and it lasts about two hours including the break. When we arrive, we will wait outside the [18]main entrance[/18] until all tickets have been collected. Stay with your group at all times, and switch your phones off before the play begins. After the show, the coach will bring us back to school, where your parents can meet you. We expect to return by about ten o'clock, but we will send a message if we are late. The total cost of the trip is [19]twelve[/19] pounds, which includes both the ticket and transport. Please give the money to your form teacher by Wednesday morning. Students who need medicine should give it to a teacher before we get on the coach. You may bring a little money for an ice cream during the break, but this is not necessary. Remember that you are representing the school, so polite behaviour is expected throughout the evening.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the group leader will be Miss Foster.
The speaker says they will leave school at five twenty.
The speaker says students should bring a small snack.
The speaker says the play is called The Lost Ticket.
The speaker says they will wait outside the main entrance.
The speaker says the total cost is twelve pounds.
